name: Tiramisu- Italian Coffee Trifle
recipeIngredient: 6 oz (1 cup) Semisweet chocolate, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the filling by beating egg yolks with sugar until light. Beat in Marsala wine.
2. Cook gently in a double boiler or in a stainless steel or glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring constantly, until thickened. Cool completely.
3. Beat mascarpone cheese until smooth and then slowly beat in the cooled custard.
4. Whip cream until light. Gently fold into the cheese mixture. Reserve.
5. Combine coffee with brandy. Reserve.
6. Line an 8-inch baking dish or trifle bowl with ladyfingers, fitting them as closely as possible; break up extras to fit into spaces. Drizzle half of the coffee mixture over the ladyfingers.
7. Spread half the filling over the ladyfingers and chocolate.
8. Repeat layers: line with remaining ladyfingers, drizzle with the remaining coffee, spread with the remaining filling, and top with the remaining chocolate.
9. Refrigerate for a few hours or overnight before serving. Tiramisu can be frozen for up to one month.
